# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

- **Initiator** (`connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with`, `connect_initiator_tls`, `connect_initiator_reconnecting`), **Acceptor** (single-session `Acceptor`) + **`AcceptorBuilder`** (multi-session routing by SessionID, `with_dynamic_template`, `allow_remotes` allow-listing, `with_tls`, `with_services`), **`run_scheduled_initiator`** (schedule-gated connect/reset/logout).
- **NO failover / multi-endpoint / `SocketConnectHost1` / alternate hosts** — reconnecting initiator uses a single `SocketAddr`.
- **NO proxy protocol** — all Proxy* keys `Unsupported("HTTP/SOCKS proxy is not yet implemented")`.
- **TLS**: initiator/acceptor take pre-built `rustls::ClientConfig`/`ServerConfig`. `NeedClientAuth`=Impl (achievable via rustls ServerConfig `with_client_auth`), `UseSNI`/`SNIHostName`=Impl, `SocketUseSSL`/`EnabledProtocols`/`CipherSuites`/`SocketKeyStore`/`SocketTrustStore`=Impl. **BUT config keys are NOT auto-parsed into rustls configs** — there is no settings→rustls builder; caller constructs rustls configs manually.
- **Socket options**: `SocketOptions { tcp_no_delay }` ONLY (TCP_NODELAY). All other socket keys (KeepAlive, ReuseAddress, Linger, buffers, OobInline, TrafficClass, SynchronousWrites) `Recognized`.
- **NO metrics exporter** — `metrics` crate is in workspace deps but NO crate lists it as a dependency; no metrics code in transport. `Monitor` provides operational status (`status`/`is_connected`/`sessions`/`force_logout`/`reset`/`send_app`) — the JMX-equivalent surface, FR-L.
- `Services { store, log, socket_options, monitor, validator }` wires MessageStore (seq continuity + reset), Log, validator (dict+opts) into Session.

### g) truefix-config — settings→SessionConfig mapping
Modules: `lib.rs`, `keys.rs`. `SessionSettings::parse` handles `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` inheritance + `${name}` interpolation → `BTreeMap<String,String>` per session.
- **NO `from_settings` / settings→`SessionConfig` mapping / builder module exists.** Config only produces string maps; nothing converts them into `SessionConfig`/`StoreConfig`/`LogConfig`/rustls. This mapping is the biggest functional gap.
- `keys.rs` is the complete Appendix A registry with `Stance` (Implemented/Recognized/Unsupported) — the authoritative "what's done" table (see below).

### h) truefix-at — scenarios (EXACT count)
`SUITE_VERSIONS = ["FIX.4.2", "FIX.4.4"]`. `server_suite()` returns **71 scenarios total** (each tagged single-version → 71 results):
- **23 per-version × 2 versions = 46**: `1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum`, `1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2c_MsgSeqNumTooLow`, `4b_ReceivedTestRequest`, `13b_UnsolicitedLogoutMessage`, `1d_LogonResponseResetFlag`, `1c_LogonAdoptsHeartBtInt`, `2f_ResendRequestGapFill`, `2f3_ResendRequestBoundedEnd`, `2_ResendRequestNotDuplicated`, `2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored`, `2x_OutOfOrderQueuedThenDrained`, `2g_SequenceResetReset`, `2d_SequenceResetGapFillAdvances`, `2e_SequenceResetGapFillBackwardIgnored`, `2h_ResendRequestNothingToResend`, `3a_ReceivedRejectConsumed`, `0a_ReceivedHeartbeatConsumed`, `2_MissingMsgSeqNum`, `0_IdleHeartbeatEmitted`, `4_TestRequestOnSilence`, `2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow`.
- **7 FIX.4.2-only field-validation**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue_42`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber_42`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType_42`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue_42`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat_42`.
- **18 FIX.4.4-only**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted`, `app_NewOrderSingleExecuted`, `app_OrdersOutboundSequenced`, `app_MessageResentAsPossDup`, `app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup`, `5_AcceptorInitiatedLogout`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat`, `2r_UnregisteredMsgType`, `special_NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, `special_LastMsgSeqNumProcessed`, `special_CheckLatencyStaleSendingTime`, `special_GarbledMessageDropped`, `special_ResendRequestChunkSize`.
- Runner (`runner.rs`): black-box, real `AcceptorBuilder` with dynamic template + dictionary validator + optional executor `Monitor` app; `SessionTweaks` toggles (enable_next_expected, enable_last_processed, check_latency, resend_chunk_size, executor_app). `run_report` runs each scenario vs each of its versions, fresh acceptor each. NO category enum — categories are just naming prefixes (1a/2b/14x/special/app).

### i) truefix facade + examples
`lib.rs` re-exports: core (`decode,encode,DecodeError,Field,FieldError,FieldMap,Group,Message,MessageCracker,MessageFactory`), session (`Action,Application,Event,Role,Session,SessionConfig,SessionId,SessionState`), transport (`connect_initiator,Acceptor,SessionHandle`, + whole `transport` module), `config` module. Does NOT re-export store/log/dict/at. **NO `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter`/`ApplicationExtended`.**
- **4 examples**: `executor.rs`, `banzai.rs`, `multi_acceptor.rs`, `ordermatch.rs` (+ `tests/examples_smoke.rs`). All set `check_latency = false` (fixtures use fixed 2024 timestamps).

### j) NEW crates/modules
No crates beyond the 9 listed. `schedule.rs` (new-ish vs a `schedule_reset.rs` that does NOT exist).

### Cross-cutting search confirmations
- **failover/alternate/SocketConnectHost1/multi-endpoint**: NONE.
- **proxy/PROXY**: NONE (Unsupported).
- **mongo/MongoDB**: NONE.
- **fixlatest/FIX Latest**: NONE.
- **DoNotSend/RejectLogon/FieldNotFound as types**: NONE.
- **Reject/BusinessReject as named error types**: NONE (only `RejectReason` enum + `ValidationError` struct + admin builder fns).
- **MessageCracker typed**: NO, still skeleton.
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter/ApplicationExtended**: NONE.
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: NOT implemented (dropped, "arrives in S3").
- **schedule_reset module**: NO (it's `schedule`).
- **from_settings/builder in config**: NONE.
- **MessageStore inside Session**: NO (BTreeMap in Session; MessageStore only in transport Services).
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored (Recognized).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: PARTIAL (corruption detection flag on FileStore; not a distinct type).
- **group parsing in decode**: NONE.
- **CheckCompID / ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder / ReverseRoute / TimeStampPrecision / PersistMessages**: NOT implemented (all Recognized).
- **metrics exporter**: NONE (no crate depends on `metrics`).
- **session latency benchmark**: NONE (only `truefix-core/benches/codec.rs`).

### Appendix A Stance summary (from keys.rs)
**Implemented**: BeginString/Sender/TargetCompID/ConnectionType/DefaultApplVerID; UseDataDictionary/DataDictionary/AppDataDictionary/TransportDataDictionary/ValidateFieldsHaveValues/ValidateUserDefinedFields/ValidateIncomingMessage/ValidateSequenceNumbers/ValidateChecksum/AllowUnknownMsgFields/CheckLatency/MaxLatency/RejectInvalidMessage; HeartBtInt/LogonTimeout/LogoutTimeout/ResetOnLogon/ResetOnLogout/ResetOnDisconnect/ResendRequestChunkSize/EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum/RequiresOrigSendingTime/AllowPosDup; StartTime/EndTime/Weekdays/TimeZone/NonStopSession; SocketAcceptAddress/Port/AcceptorTemplate/DynamicSession/AllowedRemoteAddresses; SocketConnectHost/Port/ReconnectInterval; SocketTcpNoDelay; SocketUseSSL/EnabledProtocols/CipherSuites/SocketKeyStore(+Password)/SocketTrustStore(+Password)/NeedClientAuth/UseSNI/SNIHostName; FileStorePath/FileLogPath/JdbcURL(Rec but sql feature exists).
**Unsupported**: all Proxy*, SLF4J category keys, Jndi*, Sleepycat*.
**Recognized (partial/pending)**: everything else including CheckCompID, RejectGarbledMessage, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, PersistMessages, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, TimeStampPrecision, FileStoreSync, all socket options except TcpNoDelay, all screen/file-log switches, StartDay/EndDay, RefreshOnLogon, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed.

## Biggest remaining gaps
1. **Settings→SessionConfig mapping** (config crate produces only string maps; no builder) — the #1 functional gap.
2. **Typed message codegen** still just MsgType constants; **MessageCracker** still skeleton.
3. **MessageStore not integrated into Session** (Session uses BTreeMap; store only in transport for seq continuity — sent-message resend across restarts NOT wired through the store).
4. Many validation/session keys `Recognized` but unimplemented: CheckCompID, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, RejectGarbledMessage, PersistMessages, TimeStampPrecision, ReverseRoute.
5. **No failover/multi-endpoint, no proxy, no metrics exporter, SQL is SQLite-only, CachedFileStore undifferentiated, FileStoreSync not honored.**
6. TLS config keys marked Implemented but require caller-built rustls configs (no settings→rustls auto-conversion).

### Files NOT read (low-risk, inferred from context): `truefix-core/src/codec/{mod,decode,encode}.rs`, `factory.rs`, `framing.rs`, `tags.rs`, `session/{session_id,tags,time_util}.rs`, `transport/framing.rs`, log `file/screen/composite/tracing_log/sql.rs`, and most `tests/` files. The analysis above is based on complete reads of every `lib.rs`, every primary source module, `build.rs`, `keys.rs`, all `Cargo.toml`s, all scenarios/runner, and the facade.

# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.

response.output:
# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

## EXHAUSTIVE CODEBASE ANALYSIS — TrueFix (read-only, no git history)

Workspace: 9 crates under `crates/*`, edition 2021, MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(unwrap/expect/panic/indexing)` on non-test builds. All crates at `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`. README still self-describes as "Pre-implementation (design complete)" but **code is substantially implemented** through Stage S9 (AT suite). 84 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

### a) truefix-core — types, errors, features
Modules: `codec` (mod/decode/encode), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Deps: thiserror, rust_decimal, time. Has a `codec` bench.
- **MessageCracker is STILL a skeleton** (`cracker.rs`): a single `fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>` trait, explicitly "core contract skeleton"; no typed dispatch (codegen not wired).
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp) and `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag). **NO** `DoNotSend`, `RejectLogon`, `FieldNotFound` type names exist anywhere. **NO** `Reject`/`BusinessReject` named error types in core.
- `Field` preserves raw bytes; typed accessors (`as_int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp`) never panic. `parse_utc_timestamp` accepts up to picosecond, truncates to nanosecond.
- `FieldMap` models ordered fields + nested `Member::Group` (count_tag + entries) — group *structure* exists, group *parsing from wire does NOT* (lib.rs: "decoding yields flat ordered fields").
- `Message` = header/body/trailer FieldMaps. Factory (`MessageFactory`) and `framing::frame_length` exist.

### b) truefix-dict — versions, codegen, validation
Deps: truefix-core, thiserror. Source files: `lib.rs`, `build.rs`, `fixt.rs`, `hash.rs`, `model.rs`, `parser.rs`, `validate.rs`. Bundled normalized dict sources for **FIX40, 41, 42, 43, 44, FIXT11, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2** — **NO FIX Latest**.
- **Codegen (build.rs) is STILL just MsgType constants** (`pub mod fix44_msgs { pub const NEW_ORDER_SINGLE: &str = "D"; }`) + an FNV-1a content hash (`FIX44_DICT_HASH`). **NO typed-message codegen.** Runtime asserts parsed hash == generated hash (dual-track proof, `dual_track` test).
- `ValidationOptions` (model.rs): `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types` (all bool). **NO** `CheckCompID`/`ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`/`ReverseRoute`/`TimeStampPrecision` toggles here.
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate` checks field membership/format/enumeration/required; produces `ValidationError` (a struct with `reason: RejectReason`, `ref_tag`, `business: bool`, `text`). `RejectReason` is an **enum** (InvalidTagNumber=0, RequiredTagMissing=1, TagNotDefinedForMessageType=2, UndefinedTag=3, TagSpecifiedWithoutValue=4, ValueIsIncorrect=5, IncorrectDataFormat=6, InvalidMsgType=11) — **NOT** named error types. Two rejection layers: unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject; field failures → session reject.
- `fixt.rs`: `FixtDictionaries` (transport + application dicts keyed by ApplVerID + DefaultApplVerID fallback). `parser.rs`: custom `.fixdict` text format (version/field/header/trailer/message directives). **NO `decode_with_dict` / group parsing in decode** anywhere (search confirmed empty).

### c) truefix-session — features, config, state machine
Modules: `admin`, `application`, `config`, `schedule`, `session_id`, `state`, `tags`, `time_util`. Deps: truefix-core, truefix-dict, time, async-trait.
- **`schedule.rs` exists (NOT `schedule_reset.rs`)** — `Schedule { non_stop, start_time, end_time, weekdays, utc_offset_seconds }`, `is_in_session()`, daily windows wrap past midnight, weekday filter, whole-second UTC offset (named TZ / StartDay/EndDay deferred).
- **`SessionConfig`** (config.rs) now contains: begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, **reset_on_logon (default true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency (default true), max_latency (120), logon_timeout (10), logout_timeout (10), reconnect_interval (5), schedule: Option<Schedule>**.
- **Session uses an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store, NOT the `MessageStore` trait** (state.rs). `MessageStore` is NOT integrated into Session; it's used only in transport `Services` for cross-restart sequence continuity. `seed_sequences()` lets transport hydrate seq numbers.
- `state.rs` Session methods: `new`, `set_dictionary`, `id`, `state`, `is_logged_on`, `next_out_seq`, `next_in_seq`, `seed_sequences`, `status`, `reset`, `handle`, `send_app` (+ private: hb, next_seq, send_stored, send_raw, reset_sequences, latency_ok, on_connected, maybe_next_expected, on_received, validate_app, process_in_order, drain_queue, request_resend, on_resend_request, build_resend, gap_fill, on_sequence_reset, on_logon, answer_test_request, on_logout_msg, on_start_logout, enter_disconnected, on_tick).
- Implements: logon/logout/heartbeat/test-request, **CheckLatency/MaxLatency** (stale SendingTime → Logout+Disconnect), seq-gap detection, ResendRequest (chunked via resend_request_chunk_size), SequenceReset gap-fill + reset, out-of-order queueing + drain, **NextExpectedMsgSeqNum (789)**, **LastMsgSeqNumProcessed (369)**, PossDup too-low handling, ResetSeqNumFlag on logon, Reject(35=3) + BusinessMessageReject(35=j) via `validate_app`/`admin`.
- **RejectGarbledMessage is NOT implemented** — garbled frames dropped silently in transport (comment: "arrives in S3"). **CheckCompID NOT implemented** (Recognized only). **ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder NOT implemented** (Recognized). **PersistMessages NOT honored** (Recognized). **TimeStampPrecision NOT honored** (Recognized). **ReverseRoute**: not found anywhere.
- `Application` trait (application.rs): async, `on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app` with default no-op impls; `from_admin`/`from_app` return `Result<(),String>` (Err rejects). **NO `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter`/`ApplicationExtended`**.

### d) truefix-store — backends
Modules: `file`, `memory`, `noop`, `sql` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` async trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store`. Backends: **MemoryStore, FileStore, CachedFileStore, NoopStore, SqlStore**.
- **SQL is SQLite-only** (`sqlx::sqlite`); no multi-database (Postgres/MySQL) support. Jdbc* keys all `Recognized`.
- **FileStoreSync NOT honored** (Recognized only) — `FileStore` uses synchronous `std::fs` writes; no fsync toggle.
- **CachedFileStore is just a thin wrapper over FileStore** (shares impl; doc: "write-batching optimizations will live [here] later") — NOT meaningfully differentiated yet.
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore is PARTIAL**: `FileStore` detects + tolerates corrupt trailing body records on open, exposes `was_corrupted()` flag, and recovers the good prefix (restart_resend.rs test). But it is NOT a distinct store type and the `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore` config key is only `Recognized`.

### e) truefix-log — backends
Modules: `composite`, `file`, `screen`, `sql` (feature-gated), `tracing_log`. `Log` trait: `on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event` (infallible, best-effort). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log`. Backends: **ScreenLog, FileLog, TracingLog, CompositeLog, SqlLog (async background writer)**.
- **Log config switches NOT implemented** — `FileLogHeartbeats`, `ScreenLogShowEvents/HeartBeats/Incoming/Outgoing`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, `FileIncludeTimeStampForMessages`, `ScreenIncludeMilliseconds` are ALL `Recognized` only. The trait has no toggle surface.

### f) truefix-transport — features
Modules: `framing`, `lib.rs`. Deps: core/session/dict/store/log, tokio, tokio-rustls, rustls, async-trait, tracing, time. dev-deps include `rcgen`.

## EXHAUSTIVE CODEBASE ANALYSIS — TrueFix (read-only, no git history)

Workspace: 9 crates under `crates/*`, edition 2021, MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(unwrap/expect/panic/indexing)` on non-test builds. All crates at `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`. README still self-describes as "Pre-implementation (design complete)" but **code is substantially implemented** through Stage S9 (AT suite). 84 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

### a) truefix-core — types, errors, features
Modules: `codec` (mod/decode/encode), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Deps: thiserror, rust_decimal, time. Has a `codec` bench.
- **MessageCracker is STILL a skeleton** (`cracker.rs`): a single `fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>` trait, explicitly "core contract skeleton"; no typed dispatch (codegen not wired).
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp) and `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag). **NO** `DoNotSend`, `RejectLogon`, `FieldNotFound` type names exist anywhere. **NO** `Reject`/`BusinessReject` named error types in core.
- `Field` preserves raw bytes; typed accessors (`as_int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp`) never panic. `parse_utc_timestamp` accepts up to picosecond, truncates to nanosecond.
- `FieldMap` models ordered fields + nested `Member::Group` (count_tag + entries) — group *structure* exists, group *parsing from wire does NOT* (lib.rs: "decoding yields flat ordered fields").
- `Message` = header/body/trailer FieldMaps. Factory (`MessageFactory`) and `framing::frame_length` exist.

### b) truefix-dict — versions, codegen, validation
Deps: truefix-core, thiserror. Source files: `lib.rs`, `build.rs`, `fixt.rs`, `hash.rs`, `model.rs`, `parser.rs`, `validate.rs`. Bundled normalized dict sources for **FIX40, 41, 42, 43, 44, FIXT11, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2** — **NO FIX Latest**.
- **Codegen (build.rs) is STILL just MsgType constants** (`pub mod fix44_msgs { pub const NEW_ORDER_SINGLE: &str = "D"; }`) + an FNV-1a content hash (`FIX44_DICT_HASH`). **NO typed-message codegen.** Runtime asserts parsed hash == generated hash (dual-track proof, `dual_track` test).
- `ValidationOptions` (model.rs): `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types` (all bool). **NO** `CheckCompID`/`ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`/`ReverseRoute`/`TimeStampPrecision` toggles here.
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate` checks field membership/format/enumeration/required; produces `ValidationError` (a struct with `reason: RejectReason`, `ref_tag`, `business: bool`, `text`). `RejectReason` is an **enum** (InvalidTagNumber=0, RequiredTagMissing=1, TagNotDefinedForMessageType=2, UndefinedTag=3, TagSpecifiedWithoutValue=4, ValueIsIncorrect=5, IncorrectDataFormat=6, InvalidMsgType=11) — **NOT** named error types. Two rejection layers: unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject; field failures → session reject.
- `fixt.rs`: `FixtDictionaries` (transport + application dicts keyed by ApplVerID + DefaultApplVerID fallback). `parser.rs`: custom `.fixdict` text format (version/field/header/trailer/message directives). **NO `decode_with_dict` / group parsing in decode** anywhere (search confirmed empty).

### c) truefix-session — features, config, state machine
Modules: `admin`, `application`, `config`, `schedule`, `session_id`, `state`, `tags`, `time_util`. Deps: truefix-core, truefix-dict, time, async-trait.
- **`schedule.rs` exists (NOT `schedule_reset.rs`)** — `Schedule { non_stop, start_time, end_time, weekdays, utc_offset_seconds }`, `is_in_session()`, daily windows wrap past midnight, weekday filter, whole-second UTC offset (named TZ / StartDay/EndDay deferred).
- **`SessionConfig`** (config.rs) now contains: begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, **reset_on_logon (default true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency (default true), max_latency (120), logon_timeout (10), logout_timeout (10), reconnect_interval (5), schedule: Option<Schedule>**.
- **Session uses an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store, NOT the `MessageStore` trait** (state.rs). `MessageStore` is NOT integrated into Session; it's used only in transport `Services` for cross-restart sequence continuity. `seed_sequences()` lets transport hydrate seq numbers.
- `state.rs` Session methods: `new`, `set_dictionary`, `id`, `state`, `is_logged_on`, `next_out_seq`, `next_in_seq`, `seed_sequences`, `status`, `reset`, `handle`, `send_app` (+ private: hb, next_seq, send_stored, send_raw, reset_sequences, latency_ok, on_connected, maybe_next_expected, on_received, validate_app, process_in_order, drain_queue, request_resend, on_resend_request, build_resend, gap_fill, on_sequence_reset, on_logon, answer_test_request, on_logout_msg, on_start_logout, enter_disconnected, on_tick).
- Implements: logon/logout/heartbeat/test-request, **CheckLatency/MaxLatency** (stale SendingTime → Logout+Disconnect), seq-gap detection, ResendRequest (chunked via resend_request_chunk_size), SequenceReset gap-fill + reset, out-of-order queueing + drain, **NextExpectedMsgSeqNum (789)**, **LastMsgSeqNumProcessed (369)**, PossDup too-low handling, ResetSeqNumFlag on logon, Reject(35=3) + BusinessMessageReject(35=j) via `validate_app`/`admin`.
- **RejectGarbledMessage is NOT implemented** — garbled frames dropped silently in transport (comment: "arrives in S3"). **CheckCompID NOT implemented** (Recognized only). **ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder NOT implemented** (Recognized). **PersistMessages NOT honored** (Recognized). **TimeStampPrecision NOT honored** (Recognized). **ReverseRoute**: not found anywhere.
- `Application` trait (application.rs): async, `on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app` with default no-op impls; `from_admin`/`from_app` return `Result<(),String>` (Err rejects). **NO `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter`/`ApplicationExtended`**.

### d) truefix-store — backends
Modules: `file`, `memory`, `noop`, `sql` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` async trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store`. Backends: **MemoryStore, FileStore, CachedFileStore, NoopStore, SqlStore**.

### e) truefix-log — backends
Modules: `composite`, `file`, `screen`, `sql` (feature-gated), `tracing_log`. `Log` trait: `on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event` (infallible, best-effort). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log`. Backends: **ScreenLog, FileLog, TracingLog, CompositeLog, SqlLog (async background writer)**.

### f) truefix-transport — features
Modules: `framing`, `lib.rs`. Deps: core/session/dict/store/log, tokio, tokio-rustls, rustls, async-trait, tracing, time. dev-deps include `rcgen`.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths

## Findings

The bug is caused by `baz()` skipping null checks in the headless path.

## EXHAUSTIVE CODEBASE ANALYSIS — TrueFix (read-only, no git history)

Workspace: 9 crates under `crates/*`, edition 2021, MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(unwrap/expect/panic/indexing)` on non-test builds. All crates at `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`. README still self-describes as "Pre-implementation (design complete)" but **code is substantially implemented** through Stage S9 (AT suite). 84 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

### a) truefix-core — types, errors, features
Modules: `codec` (mod/decode/encode), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Deps: thiserror, rust_decimal, time. Has a `codec` bench.
- **MessageCracker is STILL a skeleton** (`cracker.rs`): a single `fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>` trait, explicitly "core contract skeleton"; no typed dispatch (codegen not wired).
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp) and `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag). **NO** `DoNotSend`, `RejectLogon`, `FieldNotFound` type names exist anywhere. **NO** `Reject`/`BusinessReject` named error types in core.
- `Field` preserves raw bytes; typed accessors (`as_int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp`) never panic. `parse_utc_timestamp` accepts up to picosecond, truncates to nanosecond.
- `FieldMap` models ordered fields + nested `Member::Group` (count_tag + entries) — group *structure* exists, group *parsing from wire does NOT* (lib.rs: "decoding yields flat ordered fields").
- `Message` = header/body/trailer FieldMaps. Factory (`MessageFactory`) and `framing::frame_length` exist.

### b) truefix-dict — versions, codegen, validation
Deps: truefix-core, thiserror. Source files: `lib.rs`, `build.rs`, `fixt.rs`, `hash.rs`, `model.rs`, `parser.rs`, `validate.rs`. Bundled normalized dict sources for **FIX40, 41, 42, 43, 44
